verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a sperm-specific protein with a single transmembrane domain, part of the IZUMO family critical for sperm-egg fusion. It is expressed exclusively in male germline / spermatogenic lineages and localizes to the sperm plasma membrane (equatorial region and post-acrosomal region). The protein has its own TM anchor and reaches the outer leaflet as a genuine surface component of sperm cells. However, its expression is restricted to a narrow developmental / germline compartment (spermatogenic cells and mature sperm), not present on somatic cells or non-germline tissues. This tissue/lineage restriction places it in `contextual` / `tissue_restricted_surface` rather than `yes`, per the instruction that germline-restricted surface display with its own anchor still maps to `contextual` when gated by narrow cell-type lineage.
